select_when: Performance improvement requires specific reps, structured feedback loops, and error correction rather than vague effort.
The bottleneck is a weak sub-skill, poor diagnostic habit, slow recognition pattern, or inconsistent execution routine.
Progress needs to be measurable over time against a tighter benchmark, coach review, or replayable standard.
The team knows the target capability but still lacks a disciplined way to build it reliably.
avoid_when: Practice is intense but disconnected from the real failure modes, stakes, or feedback conditions of live performance.
A genuinely novel problem is being forced into the most practiced drill because that routine is the tool already at hand.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on sub-skill improvement, replayable feedback, and reality-linked drills rather than on general motivation advice.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor training-program sprawl because the raw source is already strong on practice specificity, feedback quality, and anti-fake-drill discipline.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The typed labels compress a source that spans expertise development, teaching, and skill transfer.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that deliberate practice is strongest when performance needs specific reps, sub-skill work, and measurable improvement.

avoid_when.note: The reality-link warning is explicit; the novelty mismatch line is reviewed normalization of the entrenchment and law-of-the-instrument risks.

input_type.note: The raw source activates on capability bottlenecks and sub-skill gaps, but the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields structured, feedback-linked practice loops; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Deliberate practice clearly requires diagnosis and reflection, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
